speaker: ANTONY
text: Hark, the land bids me tread no more upon ’t.

speaker: ANTONY
text: It is ashamed to bear me. Friends, come hither.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I am so lated in the world that I

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have lost my way forever. I have a ship

speaker: ANTONY
text: Laden with gold. Take that, divide it. Fly,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And make your peace with Caesar.

speaker: ALL
text: Fly? Not we!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have fled myself and have instructed cowards

speaker: ANTONY
text: To run and show their shoulders. Friends, begone.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have myself resolved upon a course

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which has no need of you. Begone.

speaker: ANTONY
text: My treasure’s in the harbor; take it. O,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I followed that I blush to look upon!

speaker: ANTONY
text: My very hairs do mutiny, for the white

speaker: ANTONY
text: Reprove the brown for rashness, and they them

speaker: ANTONY
text: For fear and doting. Friends, begone. You shall

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have letters from me to some friends that will

speaker: ANTONY
text: Sweep your way for you. Pray you look not sad,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Nor make replies of loathness. Take the hint

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which my despair proclaims. Let that be left

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which leaves itself. To the seaside straightway!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I will possess you of that ship and treasure.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Leave me, I pray, a little—pray you, now,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Nay, do so—for indeed I have lost command.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Therefore I pray you—I’ll see you by and by.

speaker: EROS
text: Nay, gentle madam, to him, comfort him.

speaker: IRAS
text: Do, most dear queen.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Do! Why, what else?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let me sit down. O Juno!

speaker: ANTONY
text: No, no, no, no, no.

speaker: EROS
text: See you here, sir?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Oh fie, fie, fie!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Madam.

speaker: IRAS
text: Madam, O good empress!

speaker: EROS
text: Sir, sir—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Yes, my lord, yes. He at Philippi kept

speaker: ANTONY
text: His sword e’en like a dancer, while I struck

speaker: ANTONY
text: The lean and wrinkled Cassius, and ’twas I

speaker: ANTONY
text: That the mad Brutus ended. He alone

speaker: ANTONY
text: Dealt on lieutenantry, and no practice had

speaker: ANTONY
text: In the brave squares of war, yet now—no matter.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ah, stand by.

speaker: EROS
text: The Queen, my lord, the Queen.

speaker: IRAS
text: Go to him, madam; speak to him.

speaker: IRAS
text: He’s unqualitied with very shame.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Well, then, sustain me. O!

speaker: EROS
text: Most noble sir, arise. The Queen approaches.

speaker: EROS
text: Her head’s declined, and death will seize her but

speaker: EROS
text: Your comfort makes the rescue.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have offended reputation,

speaker: ANTONY
text: A most unnoble swerving.

speaker: EROS
text: Sir, the Queen.

speaker: ANTONY
text: O, whither hast them led me, Egypt? See

speaker: ANTONY
text: How I convey my shame out of thine eyes,

speaker: ANTONY
text: By looking back what I have left behind

speaker: ANTONY
text: ’Stroyed in dishonor.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, my lord, my lord,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Forgive my fearful sails! I little thought

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: You would have followed.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Egypt, thou knew’st too well

speaker: ANTONY
text: My heart was to thy rudder tied by th’ strings,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And thou shouldst tow me after. O’er my spirit

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy full supremacy thou knew’st, and that

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thy beck might from the bidding of the gods

speaker: ANTONY
text: Command me.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, my pardon!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now I must

speaker: ANTONY
text: To the young man send humble treaties, dodge

speaker: ANTONY
text: And palter in the shifts of lowness, who

speaker: ANTONY
text: With half the bulk o’ th’ world played as I pleased,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Making and marring fortunes. You did know

speaker: ANTONY
text: How much you were my conqueror, and that

speaker: ANTONY
text: My sword, made weak by my affection, would

speaker: ANTONY
text: Obey it on all cause.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Pardon, pardon!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fall not a tear, I say; one of them rates

speaker: ANTONY
text: All that is won and lost. Give me a kiss.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Even this repays me.—

speaker: ANTONY
text: We sent our schoolmaster. Is he come back?—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Love, I am full of lead.—Some wine

speaker: ANTONY
text: Within there, and our viands! Fortune knows

speaker: ANTONY
text: We scorn her most when most she offers blows.
